commands.author: MonkeyLeeT
commands.review_summary:
- service;-;handle chat_guid values are treated as DM targets for outbound allowlist checks
- service;+;group chat_guid values remain group targets
- chat_id behavior is unchanged
- DM pairing challenges no longer inject numeric chatId into the guarded send path

target.title: [codex] Avoid broad Telegram reset boundary scan
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a targeted Telegram message-cache helper for finding the latest matching message before the current message.
- Use it for `/new` and hard `/reset` session-boundary detection instead of materializing every previous cached message with `recentBefore(..., Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER)`.
- Leave the existing `recentBefore` and `around` behavior unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram `/new` and hard `/reset` session-boundary lookup no longer needs to materialize every previous cached message while still fencing conversation context after the reset boundary.

Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree on PR branch `codex/telegram-cache-context-fastpath`, using Node 26 and the actual `extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts` runtime loaded through `tsx` with grammY-shaped Telegram message objects.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a runtime proof command that seeded one Telegram thread cache with 3,011 messages: 2,500 stale messages before `/new`, the `/new` boundary at message `12500`, 509 post-reset messages, and current message `13010`; then called `cache.latestBeforeMatching(...)` and `buildTelegramConversationContext(...)`.

Evidence after fix: The runtime proof printed `{"cachedMessages":3011,"boundaryId":"12500","latestBeforeMatchingSortCalls":0,"contextIds":[13000,13001,13002,13003,13004,13005,13006,13007,13008,13009],"elapsedMs":1.42}`.

Observed result after fix: `latestBeforeMatching` found the `/new` boundary without calling `Array.prototype.toSorted`, and `buildTelegramConversationContext` returned only post-reset message IDs `13000` through `13009`; no stale pre-reset body or message ID at or before `12500` leaked into the prompt context.

What was not tested: A live Telegram bot/Desktop roundtrip was not run; this proof exercises the real Telegram cache/context runtime with Telegram-shaped messages rather than a mocked helper.

## Verification

- Runtime proof command described above
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean: no accepted/actionable findings
- `git diff --check`
